revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = |正式名称 = 岡山牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業…'
revision.text:
{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = |正式名称 = 岡山牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ株式会社]] |管理運営 = オリーブパレス |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓413−2 |電話 = 0869－34－2370 }} 牛窓オリーブ園は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]]にある[[植物園]]である。 敷地面積は約10㏊で[[オリーブ]]を[[成木]]で約2000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。 主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]、[[ミッション（オリーブ）|ミッション]]、[[マンザニロ]]、[[ルッカ（オリーブ）|ルッカ]]、[[アルベキナ]]。 ==概要== 牛窓オリーブ園の[[オリーブ]]は主に食用・化粧用として使用されていて[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所としても有名である。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、日本の[[エーゲ海]]と呼ばれる。 なお、オリーブ園内でオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。 {{節stub}} ===歴史=== 牛窓オリーブ園は1942年（昭和17年）太平洋戦争中、岡山有数の商家[[服部和一郎]]が「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と、牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が始まりと考えられている。[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/main02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より
画家 [[佐竹徳]]が昭和34年から約40年間牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けて、{{要出典範囲|その作品の約80点を[[牛窓町]]（今の[[瀬戸内市]]牛窓）に寄付した}}という[[逸話]]が残っている。 {{節stub}} ==関連項目== *[[佐竹徳]] *[[牛窓町]] *[[瀬戸内市]] *[[オリーブ]] ==参照== {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{Substub}} [[Category:日本の地理]]

{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = |正式名称 = 岡山牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ株式会社]] |管理運営 = オリーブパレス |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓413−2 |電話 = 0869－34－2370 }} '''牛窓オリーブ園'''は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]]にある[[植物園]]。 敷地面積は約10㏊で[[オリーブ]]を[[成木]]で約2000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。 主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]、[[ミッション（オリーブ）|ミッション]]、[[マンザニロ]]、[[ルッカ（オリーブ）|ルッカ]]、[[アルベキナ]]。 ==概要== 牛窓オリーブ園の[[オリーブ]]は主に食用・化粧用として使用されていて[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所としても有名である。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、日本の[[エーゲ海]]と呼ばれる。 なお、オリーブ園内でオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。 {{節stub}} ===歴史=== 牛窓オリーブ園は1942年（昭和17年）太平洋戦争中、岡山有数の商家[[服部和一郎]]が「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と、牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が始まりと考えられている。[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/main02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より
画家 [[佐竹徳]]が昭和34年から約40年間牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けて、{{要出典範囲|その作品の約80点を[[牛窓町]]（今の[[瀬戸内市]]牛窓）に寄付した}}という[[逸話]]が残っている。 {{節stub}} ==関連項目== *[[佐竹徳]] *[[牛窓町]] *[[瀬戸内市]] *[[オリーブ]] ==参照== {{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}} {{Substub}} [[Category:日本の植物園]] [[Category:瀬戸内市]]

revision.comment: 長らく出典を示すことができない記述を除去しました。
revision.text:
{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = |正式名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ株式会社]] |管理運営 = 牛窓オリーブショップ |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓412−1 }} '''牛窓オリーブ園'''（うしまどオリーブえん）は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓]]にある[[植物園]]。
敷地面積は約10㏊で[[オリーブ]]を成木で約2000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。 主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]・[[ミッション (オリーブ)|ミッション]]・[[マンザニロ]]・[[ルッカ (オリーブ)|ルッカ]]・[[アルベキナ]]などがある。
==概要== 牛窓オリーブ園のオリーブは主に食用・化粧用として使用されていて[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所としても有名である。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、日本の[[エーゲ海]]と呼ばれる。
なお、オリーブ園内でオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。 {{節stub}}
===歴史=== 牛窓オリーブ園は1942年（昭和17年）太平洋戦争中、岡山有数の商家[[服部和一郎]]が「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と、牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が始まりと考えられている<ref>[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/about02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より</ref>。
画家 [[佐竹徳]]が昭和34年から約40年間牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けた。 {{節stub}}

revision.text:
{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = [[File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-a.JPG|300px]] |正式名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ]]株式会社 |管理運営 = 牛窓オリーブショップ |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓412−1 }} '''牛窓オリーブ園'''（うしまどオリーブえん）は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓町]]にある[[植物園]]。[[日本オリーブ]]株式会社が運営している。
敷地面積は約10ヘクタール。[[オリーブ]]の成木約2,000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]・[[ミッション (オリーブ)|ミッション]]・[[マンザニロ]]・[[ルッカ (オリーブ)|ルッカ]]・[[アルベキナ]]など。
== 特徴 == 牛窓オリーブ園のオリーブは主に食用・化粧用として使用されている。[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所でもある。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、「日本の[[エーゲ海]]」と呼ばれる。
オリーブ園内ではオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。
2018年（平成30年）3月24日に[[牛窓港]]一帯は[[みなとオアシス]]の登録をしていて、当園は'''みなとオアシス牛窓'''を構成する施設の一である。
== 歴史 == 岡山有数の商人だった[[服部和一郎]]は、「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と考えた。[[太平洋戦争]]中の1942年（昭和17年）、服部が牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が今日の牛窓オリーブ園の始まりと考えられている<ref>[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/about02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より</ref>。
画家の[[佐竹徳]]はオリーブ園内にアトリエを構えており、1959年（昭和34年）から約40年間にわたって牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けた。 {{節stub}}
<gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|牛窓オリーブショップ File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|牛窓オリーブ園から見た多島海 File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|牛窓市街地から見た牛窓オリーブ園 </gallery>
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Ushimado Olive Garden}} * [http://nippon-olive.info/ 公式サイト] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html みなとオアシス牛窓] 中国地方整備局

revision.comment: /* 歴史 */ 画像追加。
revision.text:
{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = [[File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-a.JPG|300px]] |正式名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ]]株式会社 |管理運営 = 牛窓オリーブショップ |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓412−1 }} '''牛窓オリーブ園'''（うしまどオリーブえん）は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓町]]にある[[植物園]]。[[日本オリーブ]]株式会社が運営している。
敷地面積は約10ヘクタール。[[オリーブ]]の成木約2,000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]・[[ミッション (オリーブ)|ミッション]]・[[マンザニロ]]・[[ルッカ (オリーブ)|ルッカ]]・[[アルベキナ]]など。
== 特徴 == 牛窓オリーブ園のオリーブは主に食用・化粧用として使用されている。[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所でもある。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、「日本の[[エーゲ海]]」と呼ばれる。
オリーブ園内ではオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。
2018年（平成30年）3月24日に[[牛窓港]]一帯は[[みなとオアシス]]の登録をしていて、当園は'''みなとオアシス牛窓'''を構成する施設の一である。
== 歴史 == 岡山有数の商人だった[[服部和一郎]]は、「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と考えた。[[太平洋戦争]]中の1942年（昭和17年）、服部が牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が今日の牛窓オリーブ園の始まりと考えられている<ref>[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/about02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より</ref>。
画家の[[佐竹徳]]はオリーブ園内にアトリエを構えており、1959年（昭和34年）から約40年間にわたって牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けた。 {{節stub}}
<gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|牛窓オリーブショップ File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|牛窓オリーブ園から見た多島海 File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|牛窓市街地から見た牛窓オリーブ園 File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|園内にある阿弥陀山古墳群 </gallery>
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Ushimado Olive Garden}} * [http://nippon-olive.info/ 公式サイト] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html みなとオアシス牛窓] 中国地方整備局
{{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしまとおりいふえん}} [[Category:日本の植物園]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の建築物]]

revision.comment: 牛窓オリーブ園の歴史を書籍や新聞記事により補完した。
revision.text:
{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = [[File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-a.JPG|300px]] |正式名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ]]株式会社 |管理運営 = 牛窓オリーブショップ |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓412−1 }} '''牛窓オリーブ園'''（うしまどオリーブえん）は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓町]]にある[[植物園]]。[[日本オリーブ]]株式会社が運営している。
敷地面積は約10ヘクタール。[[オリーブ]]の成木約2,000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]・[[ミッション (オリーブ)|ミッション]]・[[マンザニロ]]・[[ルッカ (オリーブ)|ルッカ]]・[[アルベキナ]]など。
== 特徴 == 牛窓オリーブ園のオリーブは主に食用・化粧用として使用されている。[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所でもある。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、「日本の[[エーゲ海]]」と呼ばれる。
毎年10月第四土曜日に「牛窓オリーブ収穫祭」が行われる。オリーブの収穫体験、搾油実演やテイスティング、大鍋パエリアなどオリーブ料理などオリーブに関する催しが行われ、入場は無料。2020年は第27回だったが、開催中止となり、動画配信イベントとなった。
オリーブ園内ではオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。
牛窓オリーブ園産のオリーブを使用したエキストラバージンオリーブオイルは2種ある。
ニューヨーク国際オリーブオイルコンテスト2021では「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまどスペリオル」「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまど」とも金賞を受賞。
2018年（平成30年）3月24日に[[牛窓港]]一帯は[[みなとオアシス]]の登録をしていて、当園は'''みなとオアシス牛窓'''を構成する施設の一である。
== 歴史 == 岡山有数の商人だった[[服部和一郎]]は、「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と考えた。[[太平洋戦争]]中の1942年（昭和17年）、服部が牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が今日の牛窓オリーブ園の始まりと考えられている<ref>[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/about02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より</ref>。
第二次世界大戦中、軍部から食糧増産のために所有する山林を畑に開墾するよう要請があった。その要請に服部和一郎は山をすべて畑にすると緑が失われると憂い、開墾した一部にオリーブの栽培を考えた。オリーブは香川県の小豆島で栽培されており、牛窓でも可能だと確信した。1942年、瀬戸内を見下ろす阿弥陀山の松林を開墾し、小豆島から約30本の苗木を植え、やがて800本までに増やした。「創業100年企業の経営理念」入野和生 より
牛窓オリーブ園内の「幸福の鐘」は会社創立50周年を記念して設置されたもの。
JAPAN牛窓国際芸術祭は1984年より9回開催。事業資金のほとんどは服部恒雄の個人資金で運営された。
画家の[[佐竹徳]]はオリーブ園内にアトリエを構えており、1959年（昭和34年）から約40年間にわたって牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けた。
牛窓町がエリアとして恋人の聖地に認定され、2012年8月1日に認定プレートが牛窓オリーブ園内に設置された。
{{節stub}}
<gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|牛窓オリーブショップ File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|牛窓オリーブ園から見た多島海 File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|牛窓市街地から見た牛窓オリーブ園 File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|園内にある阿弥陀山古墳群 </gallery>
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Ushimado Olive Garden}} * [http://nippon-olive.info/ 公式サイト] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html みなとオアシス牛窓] 中国地方整備局
{{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしまとおりいふえん}} [[Category:日本の植物園]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の建築物]]

牛窓オリーブ園産のオリーブを使用したエキストラバージンオリーブオイルは2種ある。
ニューヨーク国際オリーブオイルコンテスト2021では「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまどスペリオル」「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまど」とも金賞を受賞。

第二次世界大戦中、軍部から食糧増産のために所有する山林を畑に開墾するよう要請があった。その要請に服部和一郎は山をすべて畑にすると緑が失われると憂い、開墾した一部にオリーブの栽培を考えた。オリーブは香川県の小豆島で栽培されており、牛窓でも可能だと確信した。1942年、瀬戸内を見下ろす阿弥陀山の松林を開墾し、小豆島から約30本の苗木を植え、やがて800本までに増やした。「創業100年企業の経営理念」入野和生 より
牛窓オリーブ園内の「幸福の鐘」は会社創立50周年を記念して設置されたもの。
1984年よりJAPAN牛窓国際芸術祭が行われ、牛窓オリーブ園は主会場となった。事業資金のほとんどは牛窓オリーブ園創始者服部和一郎の長男である服部恒雄の個人資金で運営された。
画家の[[佐竹徳]]はオリーブ園内にアトリエを構えており、1959年（昭和34年）から約40年間にわたって牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けた。

revision.comment: アクセスや周辺情報を追加した。
revision.text:

== 施設 ==
* 山頂施設１F オリーブショップ * 山頂施設２F 山の上のロースタリ（カフェ）
== 交通アクセス ==
* 車で「岡山ブルーライン」邑久ICから県道39号線を牛窓方面に約20分。 * 岡山空港から車で約70分。 * 岡山駅から車で約50分。 * JR赤穂線邑久駅から、東備バス「牛窓行き」乗車。オリーブ園入り口下車（約20分）。そこから徒歩約30分。
== 周辺 ==
* 牛窓国際交流ヴィラ（レストランacca） * 牛窓ジェラード工房COPiO * ピクニックレストランポラリス

{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = [[File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-a.JPG|300px]] |正式名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ]]株式会社 |管理運営 = 牛窓オリーブショップ |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓412−1 }} '''牛窓オリーブ園'''（うしまどオリーブえん）は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓町]]にある[[植物園]]。[[日本オリーブ]]株式会社が運営している。
敷地面積は約10ヘクタール。[[オリーブ]]の成木約2,000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]・[[ミッション (オリーブ)|ミッション]]・[[マンザニロ]]・[[ルッカ (オリーブ)|ルッカ]]・[[アルベキナ]]など。
== 特徴 == 牛窓オリーブ園のオリーブは主に食用・化粧用として使用されている。[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所でもある。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、「日本の[[エーゲ海]]」と呼ばれる。
毎年10月第四土曜日に「牛窓オリーブ収穫祭」が行われる。オリーブの収穫体験、搾油実演やテイスティング、大鍋パエリアなどオリーブ料理などオリーブに関する催しが行われ、入場は無料。2020年は第27回だったが、新型コロナウィルス感染症による社会情勢に配慮され、開催中止となり、動画配信イベントとなった。
オリーブ園内ではオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。
牛窓オリーブ園産のオリーブを使用したエキストラバージンオリーブオイルは2種ある。
ニューヨーク国際オリーブオイルコンテスト2021では「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまどスペリオル」「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまど」とも金賞を受賞。
2018年（平成30年）3月24日に[[牛窓港]]一帯は[[みなとオアシス]]の登録をしていて、当園は'''みなとオアシス牛窓'''を構成する施設の一である。
== 歴史 == 岡山有数の商人だった[[服部和一郎]]は、「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と考えた。[[太平洋戦争]]中の1942年（昭和17年）、服部が牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が今日の牛窓オリーブ園の始まりと考えられている<ref>[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/about02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より</ref>。
第二次世界大戦中、軍部から食糧増産のために所有する山林を畑に開墾するよう要請があった。その要請に服部和一郎は山をすべて畑にすると緑が失われると憂い、開墾した一部にオリーブの栽培を考えた。オリーブは香川県の小豆島で栽培されており、牛窓でも可能だと確信した。1942年、瀬戸内を見下ろす阿弥陀山の松林を開墾し、小豆島から約30本の苗木を植え、やがて800本までに増やした。「創業100年企業の経営理念」入野和生 より
牛窓オリーブ園内の「幸福の鐘」は会社創立50周年を記念して設置されたもの。
1984年よりJAPAN牛窓国際芸術祭が行われ、牛窓オリーブ園は主会場となった。事業資金のほとんどは牛窓オリーブ園創始者服部和一郎の長男である服部恒雄の個人資金で運営された。
画家の[[佐竹徳]]はオリーブ園内にアトリエを構えており、1959年（昭和34年）から約40年間にわたって牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けた。
牛窓町がエリアとして恋人の聖地に認定され、2012年8月1日に認定プレートが牛窓オリーブ園内に設置された。
{{節stub}}
<gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|牛窓オリーブショップ File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|牛窓オリーブ園から見た多島海 File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|牛窓市街地から見た牛窓オリーブ園 File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|園内にある阿弥陀山古墳群 </gallery>
== 施設 ==
* 山頂施設１F オリーブショップ * 山頂施設２F 山の上のロースタリ（カフェ）
== 交通アクセス ==
* 車で「岡山ブルーライン」邑久ICから県道39号線を牛窓方面に約20分。 * 岡山空港から車で約70分。 * 岡山駅から車で約50分。 * JR赤穂線邑久駅から、東備バス「牛窓行き」乗車。オリーブ園入り口下車（約20分）。そこから徒歩約30分。
== 周辺 ==
* 牛窓国際交流ヴィラ（レストランacca） * 牛窓ジェラート工房COPiO * ピクニックレストランポラリス
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Ushimado Olive Garden}} * [http://nippon-olive.info/ 公式サイト] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html みなとオアシス牛窓] 中国地方整備局

It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]]. Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. revision.text:
{{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}
== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* **1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:** Olive Shop * **2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:** Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* **By car:** Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * **From Okayama Airport:** About 70 minutes by car. * **From Okayama Station:** About 50 minutes by car. * **By bus:** From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Burial Mounds within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://nippon-olive.info/ Official Website] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html Minato Oasis Ushimado - Chugoku Regional Bureau]
[[Category:Botanical gardens in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Setouchi, Okayama]] [[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]]

{{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}
== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].

The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.

From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.

revision.text:
{{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}
== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Burial Mounds within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://nippon-olive.info/ Official Website] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html Minato Oasis Ushimado - Chugoku Regional Bureau]

The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.

{{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}
== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.

== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."

The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.

From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.

{{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}
== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris

== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Burial Mounds within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://nippon-olive.info/ Official Website] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html Minato Oasis Ushimado - Chugoku Regional Bureau]

In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==

revision.text:
{{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}
== Overview ==
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Burial Mounds within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Burial Mounds within the garden </gallery>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "—>" to "-->"
revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Burial Mounds within the garden </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Burial Mounds within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris

revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Kofuns within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://nippon-olive.info/ Official Website] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html Minato Oasis Ushimado - Chugoku Regional Bureau] [[Category:Botanical gardens in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Setouchi, Okayama]] {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:qq]] <!--Ushimado Olive Garden-->[[Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]]{{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} <!--Ushimado Olive Garden-->[[Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]]{{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} <!--Ushimado Olive Garden-->[[Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]]{{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} <!--Ushimado Olive Garden-->[[Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]]{{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} [[Category:11]][[en:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:11]] [[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{植物園 |名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |画像 = [[File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-a.JPG|300px]] |正式名称 = 牛窓オリーブ園 |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[オリーブ]] |事業主体 = [[日本オリーブ]]株式会社 |管理運営 = 牛窓オリーブショップ |開園 = |所在地郵便番号 = |所在地 = 岡山県瀬戸内市牛窓町牛窓412−1 }} '''牛窓オリーブ園'''（うしまどオリーブえん）は[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓町]]にある[[植物園]]。[[日本オリーブ]]株式会社が運営している。
敷地面積は約10ヘクタール。[[オリーブ]]の成木約2,000本を栽培しており、[[小豆島オリーブ園]]と並び日本最大規模を誇る。主要栽培品種は[[ネバディロブランコ]]・[[ミッション (オリーブ)|ミッション]]・[[マンザニロ]]・[[ルッカ (オリーブ)|ルッカ]]・[[アルベキナ]]など。
== 特徴 == 牛窓オリーブ園のオリーブは主に食用・化粧用として使用されている。[[瀬戸内市]]の特産であると共に、オリーブ園から見えるその美しい景観から観光名所でもある。特に牛窓オリーブ園山頂広場からの眺望は、「日本の[[エーゲ海]]」と呼ばれる。
毎年10月第四土曜日に「牛窓オリーブ収穫祭」が行われる。オリーブの収穫体験、搾油実演やテイスティング、大鍋パエリアなどオリーブ料理などオリーブに関する催しが行われ、入場は無料。2020年は第27回だったが、新型コロナウィルス感染症による社会情勢に配慮され、開催中止となり、動画配信イベントとなった。
オリーブ園内ではオリーブ製品を買うことが出来る。
牛窓オリーブ園産のオリーブを使用したエキストラバージンオリーブオイルは2種ある。
ニューヨーク国際オリーブオイルコンテスト2021では「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまどスペリオル」「エキストラバージンオリーブオイルうしまど」とも金賞を受賞。
2018年（平成30年）3月24日に[[牛窓港]]一帯は[[みなとオアシス]]の登録をしていて、当園は'''みなとオアシス牛窓'''を構成する施設の一である。
== 歴史 == 岡山有数の商人だった[[服部和一郎]]は、「オリーブは栄養がよく、薬用にもなり、灯火にもなり、自然も守る」と考えた。[[太平洋戦争]]中の1942年（昭和17年）、服部が牛窓の丘陵地に作ったオリーブ畑が今日の牛窓オリーブ園の始まりと考えられている<ref>[http://www.nippon-olive.co.jp/about/about02.htm 日本オリーブ株式会社ホームページ]より</ref>。
第二次世界大戦中、軍部から食糧増産のために所有する山林を畑に開墾するよう要請があった。その要請に服部和一郎は山をすべて畑にすると緑が失われると憂い、開墾した一部にオリーブの栽培を考えた。オリーブは香川県の小豆島で栽培されており、牛窓でも可能だと確信した。1942年、瀬戸内を見下ろす阿弥陀山の松林を開墾し、小豆島から約30本の苗木を植え、やがて800本までに増やした。「創業100年企業の経営理念」入野和生 より
牛窓オリーブ園内の「幸福の鐘」は会社創立50周年を記念して設置されたもの。
1984年よりJAPAN牛窓国際芸術祭が行われ、牛窓オリーブ園は主会場となった。事業資金のほとんどは牛窓オリーブ園創始者服部和一郎の長男である服部恒雄の個人資金で運営された。
画家の[[佐竹徳]]はオリーブ園内にアトリエを構えており、1959年（昭和34年）から約40年間にわたって牛窓オリーブ園の絵を描き続けた。
牛窓町がエリアとして恋人の聖地に認定され、2012年8月1日に認定プレートが牛窓オリーブ園内に設置された。
{{節stub}}
<gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|牛窓オリーブショップ File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|牛窓オリーブ園から見た多島海 File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|牛窓市街地から見た牛窓オリーブ園 File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|園内にある阿弥陀山古墳群 </gallery>
== 施設 ==
* 山頂施設１F オリーブショップ * 山頂施設２F 山の上のロースタリ（カフェ）
== 交通アクセス ==
* 車で「岡山ブルーライン」邑久ICから県道39号線を牛窓方面に約20分。 * 岡山空港から車で約70分。 * 岡山駅から車で約50分。 * JR赤穂線邑久駅から、東備バス「牛窓行き」乗車。オリーブ園入り口下車（約20分）。そこから徒歩約30分。
== 周辺 ==
* 牛窓国際交流ヴィラ（レストランacca） * 牛窓ジェラート工房COPiO * ピクニックレストランポラリス
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Ushimado Olive Garden}} * [http://nippon-olive.info/ 公式サイト] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html みなとオアシス牛窓] 中国地方整備局
{{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしまとおりいふえん}} [[Category:日本の植物園]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Kofuns within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://nippon-olive.info/ Official Website] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html Minato Oasis Ushimado - Chugoku Regional Bureau] {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Olive Garden}}

}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{translated page|" to "[[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|"
revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:11]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[Arbequina]], [[Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[Manzanillo]], [[Frantoio]], and [[Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Kofuns within the garden </gallery>

Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.

* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
