Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.

revision.text:
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[Setouchi]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[:en:Arbequina|Arbequina]], [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[:en:Manzanillo|Manzanillo]], [[:en:Frantoio|Frantoio]], and [[:en:Leccino|Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[:en:Aegean Sea|Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."

The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.

Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.

The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.

It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[:en:Arbequina|Arbequina]], [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[:en:Manzanillo|Manzanillo]], [[:en:Frantoio|Frantoio]], and [[:en:Leccino|Leccino]].
== Features ==

Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[:en:Aegean Sea|Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4333775]] Wikidata name: Ushimado Olive Garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[:en:Setouchi|Setouchi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[:en:Arbequina|Arbequina]], [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[:en:Manzanillo|Manzanillo]], [[:en:Frantoio|Frantoio]], and [[:en:Leccino|Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[:en:Aegean Sea|Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Kofuns within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://nippon-olive.info/ Official Website] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html Minato Oasis Ushimado - Chugoku Regional Bureau]

From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.

* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4333775]] Wikidata name: Ushimado Olive Garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[:en:Setouchi|Setouchi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[:en:Arbequina|Arbequina]], [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[:en:Manzanillo|Manzanillo]], [[:en:Frantoio|Frantoio]], and [[:en:Leccino|Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[:en:Aegean Sea|Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris

* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4333775]] Wikidata name: Ushimado Olive Garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[:en:Setouchi|Setouchi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[:en:Arbequina|Arbequina]], [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[:en:Manzanillo|Manzanillo]], [[:en:Frantoio|Frantoio]], and [[:en:Leccino|Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[:en:Aegean Sea|Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[commons:" to "[[co:commons:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4333775]] Wikidata name: Ushimado Olive Garden [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in [[:en:Setouchi|Setouchi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include [[:en:Arbequina|Arbequina]], [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], [[:en:Manzanillo|Manzanillo]], [[:en:Frantoio|Frantoio]], and [[:en:Leccino|Leccino]].
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's [[:en:Aegean Sea|Aegean Sea]]."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Ushimado olive land observation deck-b.JPG|Ushimado Olive Shop File:Ushimado olive land-a.JPG|Multitude of islands seen from Ushimado Olive Garden File:Ushimado olive land-e.JPG|Ushimado Olive Garden as viewed from Ushimado Town File:Amidayama_Kofun_Group,_gaikan.jpg|Amida Mountain Kofuns within the garden </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://nippon-olive.info/ Official Website] * [http://www.pa.cgr.mlit.go.jp/minatooasis/ushimado/index.html Minato Oasis Ushimado - Chugoku Regional Bureau]

* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]]
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in {{ill|Setouchi|en|Setouchi|WD=Q1055922|lt=Setouchi}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include {{ill|Arbequina|en|Arbequina|WD=Q2859651|lt=Arbequina}}, [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], {{ill|Manzanillo|en|Manzanillo|WD=Q222571|lt=Manzanillo}}, {{ill|Frantoio|en|Frantoio|WD=Q3083056|lt=Frantoio}}, and {{ill|Leccino|en|Leccino|WD=Q3228722|lt=Leccino}}.
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's {{ill|Aegean Sea|en|Aegean Sea|WD=Q34575|lt=Aegean Sea}}."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris

From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Ushimado Olive Garden]][[ja:牛窓オリーブ園]][[ru:Оливковый сад Усимадо]]
'''Ushimado Olive Garden''' (牛窓オリーブ園) is a botanical garden located in {{ill|Setouchi|en|Setouchi|WD=Q1055922|lt=Setouchi|1=Setouchi}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, specifically in Ushimado Town. It is operated by the [[Japan Olive Co., Ltd.]].
Covering an area of approximately 10 hectares, the garden cultivates around 2,000 mature olive trees, making it one of Japan's largest olive groves alongside [[Shodoshima Olive Garden]]. The main cultivated varieties include {{ill|Arbequina|en|Arbequina|WD=Q2859651|lt=Arbequina|1=Arbequina}}, [[:en:Mission (olive)|Mission]], {{ill|Manzanillo|en|Manzanillo|WD=Q222571|lt=Manzanillo|1=Manzanillo}}, {{ill|Frantoio|en|Frantoio|WD=Q3083056|lt=Frantoio|1=Frantoio}}, and {{ill|Leccino|en|Leccino|WD=Q3228722|lt=Leccino|1=Leccino}}.
== Features ==
The olives from Ushimado Olive Garden are primarily used for culinary and cosmetic purposes. Besides being a specialty of Setouchi City, the garden is also a famous tourist destination known for its stunning scenery. The view from the hilltop plaza is particularly renowned, earning the moniker "Japan's {{ill|Aegean Sea|en|Aegean Sea|WD=Q34575|lt=Aegean Sea|1=Aegean Sea}}."
Every year on the fourth Saturday of October, the "Ushimado Olive Harvest Festival" is held. Activities include olive picking, oil extraction demonstrations, tastings, and olive-themed dishes such as a giant paella. Admission is free. In 2020, the 27th festival was canceled due to COVID-19 and replaced with an online event.
The garden's shop sells various olive products. Two types of extra virgin olive oil made from Ushimado olives have won gold awards at the 2021 New York International Olive Oil Competition: "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado Superior" and "Extra Virgin Olive Oil Ushimado."
Since March 24, 2018, the Ushimado Port area has been registered as a [[Minato Oasis]], and the garden is a key facility of the "Minato Oasis Ushimado."
== History ==
The garden traces its origins to 1942 when Kazutaro Hattori, a prominent merchant in Okayama, planted olive trees on the hills of Ushimado during World War II. Believing olives were nutritious, medicinal, and environmentally protective, he resisted government directives to convert his entire forested land into farmland, instead dedicating a portion to olive cultivation.
Approximately 30 seedlings from Shodoshima were planted on Mount Amida, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea, eventually growing into an olive grove of 800 trees.
A "Bell of Happiness" was installed within the garden to commemorate the company's 50th anniversary.
From 1984, the Japan Ushimado International Art Festival was held, with the garden serving as the main venue. It was largely funded by Hattori’s eldest son, Tsuneo Hattori.
Renowned painter [[Norio Satake]] maintained a studio within the garden, dedicating 40 years from 1959 to painting its landscapes.
In 2012, Ushimado Town was designated a "Sacred Place for Lovers," with a commemorative plaque installed in the garden on August 1.
== Facilities ==
* '''1st Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Olive Shop * '''2nd Floor of the Hilltop Facility:''' Mountain Roastery Café
== Access ==
* '''By car:''' Approximately 20 minutes from the Oku IC on the Okayama Blue Line via Route 39 towards Ushimado. * '''From Okayama Airport:''' About 70 minutes by car. * '''From Okayama Station:''' About 50 minutes by car. * '''By bus:''' From JR Ako Line's Oku Station, take the Tobibus to Ushimado and alight at the Olive Garden Entrance (20 minutes). From there, it's about a 30-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* Ushimado International Exchange Villa (Restaurant Acca) * Ushimado Gelato Workshop COPiO * Picnic Restaurant Polaris
